entries.title: Sirius Black
entries.text: Sirius Black is godfather to Harry Potter and was lifelong friend to James Potter. Born into the ancient and noble House of Black, he openly rejected the family's pureblood supremacy as a teenager and ran away from home at sixteen. He was wrongfully imprisoned in Azkaban for twelve years on charges of betraying the Potters and murdering thirteen Muggles, both crimes actually committed by Peter Pettigrew. Sirius escaped Azkaban in 1993 and remains a fugitive of the Ministry of Magic, sheltering at his ancestral home, 12 Grimmauld Place. He is an Animagus who can transform into a large black dog.

entries.title: The Two-Way Mirror
entries.text: The two-way mirror is a paired magical object that allows two holders to see and speak with each other across any distance. Sirius gave Harry one of the pair as a Christmas gift in his fifth year, with a note inside the package telling Harry to use it if he needed to reach Sirius. Harry, unaware of what the package contained, did not open it until much later. The mirror's communication is private and direct — it bypasses owl post and Floo, and cannot be intercepted by the Ministry or by Umbridge. It is one of the few reliable channels between Harry at Hogwarts and Sirius at Grimmauld Place.

entries.title: 12 Grimmauld Place
entries.text: Number 12, Grimmauld Place is the ancestral home of the Black family in London. Hidden under a Fidelius Charm and serving as headquarters of the Order of the Phoenix during the Second Wizarding War, it is unplottable from the outside and accessible only to those given the secret by its Secret-Keeper. Sirius inherited the house in 1995. The house is haunted by the screaming portrait of Walburga Black, Sirius's mother, and tended by Kreacher, the family's resentful house-elf.

entries.title: The Department of Mysteries
entries.text: The Department of Mysteries is a sealed division of the Ministry of Magic, located deep beneath Whitehall in London. Its researchers, called Unspeakables, study time, thought, death, love, and prophecy. Each subject is housed in its own chamber — the Time Room, the Brain Room, the Hall of Prophecies, the Death Chamber — connected by a circular antechamber whose doors rotate when entered, disorienting all who pass through. Most Unspeakables work in lifetime secrecy, and even other Ministry employees rarely know what is studied below.

entries.title: The Veil in the Death Chamber
entries.text: In the Death Chamber, at the lowest level of the Department of Mysteries, stands a stone archway draped with a tattered, motionless veil. The veil sways without wind. Those who stand near it have reported hearing voices — whispers calling them through. To pass through the archway is to die: the body does not return, and no spell raised on the other side has ever been answered. Unspeakables believe the Veil is a true threshold between life and death, perhaps the only such threshold in the wizarding world.

entries.title: The Hall of Prophecies
entries.text: The Hall of Prophecies is one of the largest chambers in the Department of Mysteries — towering shelves of small glass orbs, each containing a recorded prophecy. The orbs are organised by date and subject, glowing faintly in the dim light. A prophecy may be lifted only by the witch or wizard about whom it was made; for any other person to take it from the shelf is permitted only by force or trickery. Most prophecies are minor and unfulfilled, gathered for centuries and forgotten.

entries.title: Bellatrix Lestrange
entries.text: Bellatrix Lestrange, born Bellatrix Black, is first cousin to Sirius Black and the most fanatical of Voldemort's Death Eaters. She was imprisoned in Azkaban for the torture of Frank and Alice Longbottom, parents of Harry's classmate Neville. She broke out in the mass escape of 1996 and rejoined her master immediately. Bellatrix is a duellist of extreme skill, particularly accomplished in the Cruciatus Curse, and she takes personal pleasure in cruelty. She regards Sirius as a blood traitor and would relish any opportunity to kill him.

entries.title: The Order of the Phoenix
entries.text: The Order of the Phoenix is a secret society founded by Albus Dumbledore to oppose Lord Voldemort. Disbanded after Voldemort's first fall in 1981, it was reconvened in 1995 when Voldemort returned. Its members include Aurors, former students, parents, and friends — Sirius Black, Remus Lupin, Nymphadora Tonks, Kingsley Shacklebolt, Severus Snape, and Mad-Eye Moody among them. The Order operates from 12 Grimmauld Place, communicates through Patronus messengers, and works largely outside Ministry sanction, since the Ministry refuses to acknowledge that Voldemort has returned.

entries.title: Kreacher
entries.text: Kreacher is the house-elf of 12 Grimmauld Place, bound to the Black family for generations. Aged and bitter, he loathes Sirius — whom he regards as a blood traitor — and reveres the memory of Walburga Black, Sirius's mother, whose pureblood values he still serves. By the rules of house-elf magic, Kreacher must obey his master's direct orders, but he is free to interpret loosely-worded instructions in whatever way will most harm those who give them. His resentment makes him a vulnerable point in any plan that depends on Grimmauld Place's secrecy.

entries.title: Occlumency and the Mind Connection
entries.text: Occlumency is the magical defence of the mind against external penetration. A skilled Occlumens can shield their thoughts and emotions from a Legilimens — one who reads minds. Voldemort, an exceptional Legilimens, has had a connection to Harry's mind since the curse that gave Harry his scar. Through this link Voldemort can plant feelings, dreams, and even false visions. Snape, instructed by Dumbledore, attempted to teach Harry Occlumency in his fifth year so that he could close his mind. Harry's progress was uneven, and the lessons ended in a breach of trust before the link could be closed.

entries.title: Harry Potter
entries.text: Harry Potter is the son of James and Lily Potter and the only known survivor of the Killing Curse. Voldemort's failed attempt to kill him as an infant left Harry with a lightning-shaped scar and an unintended mental connection to Voldemort. Harry was raised by the Dursleys and entered Hogwarts in 1991, where he was sorted into Gryffindor. He is brave, loyal, impulsive, and strongly driven by the desire to protect others, especially his friends and godfather Sirius Black.

entries.title: Ron Weasley
entries.text: Ron Weasley is Harry Potter's best friend and the youngest Weasley son. Raised in a loving but poor pure-blood family, Ron often feels overshadowed by his older brothers and insecure about his own worth. Despite jealousy and fear, he repeatedly chooses loyalty and courage when it matters. His knowledge of wizarding customs helps Harry understand the magical world.

entries.title: Hermione Granger
entries.text: Hermione Granger is a Muggle-born witch and one of Harry Potter's closest friends. Brilliant, disciplined, and highly logical, she often relies on research, planning, and rules to solve problems. Hermione is deeply loyal and morally driven, especially against injustice and prejudice. Her intelligence frequently prevents Harry and Ron from acting recklessly.

entries.title: Albus Dumbledore
entries.text: Albus Dumbledore is Headmaster of Hogwarts and founder of the Order of the Phoenix. He is one of the most powerful wizards alive and Voldemort's most feared opponent. Dumbledore often acts through secrecy, long-term planning, and indirect guidance. Though he cares for Harry, he withholds painful truths from him in an attempt to protect both Harry and the wider war effort.

entries.title: Lord Voldemort
entries.text: Lord Voldemort, born Tom Riddle, is a dark wizard obsessed with immortality, domination, and pure-blood supremacy despite being half-blood himself. He fears death above all things and uses terror, manipulation, and murder to control others. Voldemort lacks the ability to understand love, loyalty, and self-sacrifice, which repeatedly blinds him to the choices of his enemies.

entries.title: Severus Snape
entries.text: Severus Snape is Potions Master at Hogwarts and later Defence Against the Dark Arts professor. A former Death Eater, he secretly works for Dumbledore because of his enduring love for Lily Potter and guilt over her death. Snape despises Harry because he resembles James Potter, but he also protects him because Harry is Lily's son. His loyalties are difficult to read because he is bitter, secretive, and highly skilled at Occlumency.

entries.title: Draco Malfoy
entries.text: Draco Malfoy is a Slytherin student from a wealthy pure-blood family. Raised under Lucius Malfoy's influence, Draco values status, blood purity, and family reputation, but he is often more insecure than truly cruel. He bullies Harry and his friends, yet he struggles when forced into real violence. Draco is especially vulnerable to pressure involving his parents and the Malfoy name.

entries.title: Neville Longbottom
entries.text: Neville Longbottom is a Gryffindor student whose parents, Frank and Alice Longbottom, were tortured into insanity by Death Eaters. Neville begins as timid and forgetful, but his courage grows steadily as the war intensifies. His greatest fear is failing those who came before him, but his loyalty and moral strength make him one of Harry's most reliable allies.

entries.title: Remus Lupin
entries.text: Remus Lupin is a werewolf, former Hogwarts professor, and one of James Potter's closest friends. Calm, kind, and intelligent, he often acts as a voice of reason. His condition causes deep shame and social isolation, making him hesitant to accept happiness or responsibility. Lupin is loyal to Harry and Sirius, but his caution can prevent him from acting decisively.

entries.title: Peter Pettigrew
entries.text: Peter Pettigrew was once a friend of James Potter, Sirius Black, and Remus Lupin. Out of fear and self-preservation, he betrayed the Potters to Voldemort and framed Sirius Black for the crime. Pettigrew survived for years disguised as Ron Weasley's rat, Scabbers. He is cowardly and opportunistic, but his life debt to Harry creates a weakness in his loyalty to Voldemort.

entries.title: Lucius Malfoy
entries.text: Lucius Malfoy is Draco Malfoy's father, a wealthy pure-blood aristocrat and former Death Eater. He uses influence, money, and intimidation to maintain power within wizarding society. Lucius serves Voldemort when it benefits him, but his loyalty is tied closely to status and family survival. His failures make the Malfoy family vulnerable to Voldemort's punishment.

entries.title: Narcissa Malfoy
entries.text: Narcissa Malfoy, born Narcissa Black, is Draco Malfoy's mother and sister to Bellatrix Lestrange. Unlike Bellatrix, Narcissa's deepest loyalty is to her family rather than Voldemort. She values pure-blood status but will betray even the Dark Lord if Draco's life is at stake. Her love for her son makes her one of the few people capable of acting against Voldemort from within his own side.

entries.title: Dolores Umbridge
entries.text: Dolores Umbridge is a Ministry official who becomes Defence Against the Dark Arts professor and later High Inquisitor of Hogwarts. She hides cruelty behind politeness, rules, and bureaucratic authority. Umbridge refuses to admit Voldemort's return and punishes students who speak the truth. Her control of Hogwarts makes communication, resistance, and student organizing extremely dangerous.

entries.title: Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry
entries.text: Hogwarts is the main magical school in Britain, hidden from Muggles and protected by powerful enchantments. Students are sorted into Gryffindor, Slytherin, Ravenclaw, or Hufflepuff. The castle contains moving staircases, secret passages, enchanted portraits, hidden rooms, and ancient magic. Hogwarts is both a place of safety and a battlefield when war reaches the school.

entries.title: The Forbidden Forest
entries.text: The Forbidden Forest borders Hogwarts and contains many magical creatures, including centaurs, unicorns, giant spiders, and thestrals. Students are forbidden to enter without permission because the forest is dangerous and unpredictable. The forest often becomes a place where hidden truths, secret meetings, and dangerous encounters take place.

entries.title: Azkaban
entries.text: Azkaban is the wizarding prison guarded for many years by Dementors. Prisoners are exposed to despair, fear, and the draining effects of the Dementors, often leaving them mentally broken. Sirius Black survived twelve years there partly because he knew he was innocent, which helped him resist complete collapse. Escaping Azkaban is considered nearly impossible.

entries.title: Malfoy Manor
entries.text: Malfoy Manor is the ancestral home of the Malfoy family. During Voldemort's return, it becomes a gathering place and temporary headquarters for Death Eaters. The manor represents wealth, pure-blood pride, and fear under Voldemort's rule. Although luxurious, it becomes a prison-like place for the Malfoys once Voldemort begins using their home.

entries.title: The Fidelius Charm
entries.text: The Fidelius Charm hides a secret inside the soul of a chosen Secret-Keeper. A protected location cannot be found unless the Secret-Keeper reveals it. The charm was used to hide the Potters before their deaths and later to protect 12 Grimmauld Place. The choice of Secret-Keeper is crucial, because betrayal can destroy the protection completely.

entries.title: The Patronus Charm
entries.text: The Patronus Charm produces a guardian made of positive emotion and memory. It is the main defence against Dementors. Advanced witches and wizards can produce a corporeal Patronus in the form of an animal. Members of the Order of the Phoenix also use Patronuses as secure magical messengers.

entries.title: Horcruxes
entries.text: A Horcrux is an object or living vessel containing a fragment of a wizard's soul, created through murder and dark magic. Voldemort used Horcruxes to protect himself from death. As long as a Horcrux survives, Voldemort cannot be fully destroyed. Destroying a Horcrux usually requires extremely destructive magical forces such as basilisk venom or Fiendfyre.

entries.title: The Unforgivable Curses
entries.text: The three Unforgivable Curses are the Killing Curse, the Cruciatus Curse, and the Imperius Curse. Their use on another person is considered one of the gravest crimes in wizarding law. The Killing Curse causes instant death, the Cruciatus Curse causes extreme pain, and the Imperius Curse controls the victim's actions. Death Eaters frequently use these curses to terrify and dominate others.

entries.title: Polyjuice Potion
entries.text: Polyjuice Potion allows the drinker to temporarily take the physical form of another person, provided they have a piece of that person's body such as hair. The potion is complex and time-consuming to brew. It changes appearance but does not grant memories, knowledge, or personality. Careful acting is required for the disguise to succeed.

entries.title: Time-Turners
entries.text: A Time-Turner is a magical device that allows limited travel into the past. Time travel is dangerous because careless interference can create confusion, paradox-like consequences, or accidental exposure. Time-Turners are closely controlled by the Ministry of Magic. Their use requires precision, secrecy, and strict discipline.

entries.title: Animagi
entries.text: An Animagus is a witch or wizard who can transform into a specific animal at will while keeping their human mind. Becoming an Animagus is difficult and usually requires registration with the Ministry. James Potter, Sirius Black, and Peter Pettigrew secretly became unregistered Animagi to help Remus Lupin during his werewolf transformations.

entries.title: Werewolves
entries.text: A werewolf transforms during the full moon and becomes dangerous to humans unless protected by special measures. Wolfsbane Potion can allow a werewolf to keep their mind during transformation, but it does not prevent the transformation itself. Werewolves face severe prejudice in wizarding society, which affects Remus Lupin's choices and self-worth.

entries.title: The Death of James and Lily Potter
entries.text: James and Lily Potter were murdered by Voldemort on Halloween night in 1981 after Peter Pettigrew betrayed their hiding place. Lily's self-sacrifice protected Harry from the Killing Curse, causing Voldemort's body to be destroyed and leaving Harry with a scar. This event ended the First Wizarding War and made Harry famous as the Boy Who Lived.

entries.title: Voldemort's Return
entries.text: Voldemort returned to a physical body in 1995 after the Triwizard Tournament. The Ministry of Magic refused to acknowledge his return, leaving Harry and Dumbledore publicly discredited. This denial allowed Voldemort to rebuild power in secret while the Order of the Phoenix prepared for war outside official support.

entries.title: Dumbledore's Army
entries.text: Dumbledore's Army is a secret student group formed in Harry's fifth year to learn practical Defence Against the Dark Arts. The group forms because Umbridge refuses to teach students real defensive magic. Harry teaches spells such as the Disarming Charm, Stunning Spell, Shield Charm, and Patronus Charm. The group gives students confidence, unity, and a foundation for later resistance.

entries.title: The Battle of the Department of Mysteries
entries.text: The Battle of the Department of Mysteries occurs after Harry is manipulated into believing Sirius is being tortured at the Ministry. Harry and several friends enter the Department of Mysteries, where Death Eaters are waiting to seize the prophecy. Members of the Order arrive to rescue them. During the battle, Sirius fights Bellatrix Lestrange and falls through the Veil in the Death Chamber.

entries.title: Harry and Sirius's Relationship
entries.text: Harry sees Sirius Black as both family and a possible escape from the Dursleys. Sirius sees Harry partly as his godson and partly as a living reminder of James Potter. Their bond is emotionally intense because both are lonely, grieving, and desperate for family. This makes Harry especially vulnerable to any threat involving Sirius.

entries.title: Harry and Snape's Hostility
entries.text: Harry and Snape distrust each other from Harry's first year at Hogwarts. Snape sees James Potter's arrogance in Harry, while Harry sees Snape as cruel, biased, and possibly evil. This hostility damages Harry's Occlumency lessons and prevents him from accepting warnings that might protect him from Voldemort's manipulation.

entries.title: Sirius and Kreacher's Conflict
entries.text: Sirius Black despises 12 Grimmauld Place because it represents his family's pure-blood cruelty, and he often treats Kreacher with anger and contempt. Kreacher, loyal to the old Black family values, hates Sirius as a blood traitor. Their mutual hostility creates a serious weakness inside the Order's headquarters, because Kreacher can obey orders while still twisting them against Sirius.

entries.title: Draco and Family Pressure
entries.text: Draco Malfoy's identity is shaped by his parents, especially Lucius Malfoy's expectations of pure-blood pride and social dominance. Draco wants to prove himself worthy of the Malfoy name, but he is not emotionally prepared for the violence demanded by Voldemort. Pressure involving his family's safety can push Draco into dangerous choices or make him hesitate at critical moments.
